pubmed_links: [Mercati et al. (2017) CNTN6 mutations are risk factors for abnormal auditory sensory perception in autism spectrum disorders](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/27166760/); [Hu et al. (2015) CNTN6 copy number variations in 14 patients: a possible candidate gene for neurodevelopmental and neuropsychiatric disorders](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/26257835/); [Juan-Perez et al. (2018) Schizophrenia and epilepsy as a result of maternally inherited CNTN6 copy number variant](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/29983269/)
summary: CNTN6, a neuronal cell adhesion molecule promoting neurite outgrowth, is significantly enriched for deletions and rare coding variants in autism spectrum disorder, with carriers exhibiting hyperacusis and auditory pathway abnormalities; CNTN6 CNVs have also been reported in schizophrenia, epilepsy, and intellectual disability. (emerging evidence)
